challenges.name: fizzbuzz
challenges.description: Baseline correctness + code style
challenges.notes: Correct, clean, well-documented solution with extra ValueError handling not required by the spec but reasonable; docstring example formatting (wrapped list) is slightly misleading vs actual doctest output but harmless since not run as doctest.

challenges.name: binary-search
challenges.description: Algorithm + full docstring (Args/Returns/Raises + examples)
challenges.notes: Correct, overflow-safe binary search with clean logic, thorough docstring/comments, and good extra test coverage beyond the required 3 examples, though duplicates aren't explicitly tested.

challenges.name: refactor
challenges.description: Code clarity + whether the model can explain its changes
challenges.notes: Correct, idiomatic refactor with meaningful names, type hints, and a clear docstring/example; the explanation is specific but includes an unsubstantiated performance claim (10-15% speedup).

challenges.name: readme-writer
challenges.description: Pure documentation ability — no code at all
challenges.notes: Comprehensive, well-organized README covering all required sections with clear examples, though some invented details (hash/line-count logic, placeholder GitHub URL) go beyond the stated scope.

challenges.name: test-writing
challenges.description: Edge-case thinking + pytest parametrize discipline
challenges.notes: Comprehensive parametrized happy-path and error coverage with correct arithmetic, but the response cuts off mid-test with an unfinished/no-op assertion, and uses classes despite the 'plain functions' requirement, slightly hurting quality.

challenges.name: go-test
challenges.description: Idiomatic Go table-driven tests + benchmark
challenges.notes: Comprehensive table-driven tests with correct map comparison via maps.Equal and a proper benchmark loop, but lacks a doc comment on TestWordCount and makes some debatable assumptions about punctuation/apostrophe handling.

challenges.name: async-fetch
challenges.description: Async concurrency patterns + retry/timeout handling
challenges.notes: Solid overall (proper session reuse, per-request ClientTimeout, asyncio.gather, error containment) but backoff is linear (0.5*(n+1)) rather than truly exponential, and persistent 5xx failures after retries exhaust silently return status with error=None; retry logic is also duplicated across multiple except blocks instead of being unified.

challenges.name: sql
challenges.description: CTE + window function fluency on a real schema
challenges.notes: Logic for revenue, avg, and top_customer (via DISTINCT ON) is correct, but revenue_pct is computed with a CROSS JOIN CTE rather than an actual window function (SUM(...) OVER()), directly violating an explicit requirement despite the model's rationalization; comments and explanations are clear and well-organized otherwise.

challenges.name: elixir-test
challenges.description: ExUnit describe blocks + assert_raise + unicode handling
challenges.notes: patched after judge fix

challenges.name: debug
challenges.description: Careful reading + correctness reasoning over broken code
challenges.notes: Correctly identifies the count>1 filter and empty-string bugs and passes all test cases, but 'Bug 3' is not a distinct third defect—it's the same buggy line and fix as Bug 1 restated, so the model effectively only found 2 genuine bugs despite the task requiring 3.

challenges.name: api-client
challenges.description: Class design + error handling + type hints + docs
challenges.notes: Well-structured and thoroughly documented up to the point of truncation, but the response cuts off mid-docstring in the post method, omitting its body and the required usage example entirely, and it never uses a requests.Session for connection reuse.

challenges.name: doom
challenges.description: DDA raycaster + textures + door + minimap + Z-buffer — the signature challenge
challenges.notes: Response is severely truncated mid-function, leaving out nearly all rendering, player, HUD, and code logic — completely unusable and unscoreable as a working implementation.

challenges.name: fizzbuzz
challenges.description: Baseline correctness + code style
challenges.notes: Correct, clean, idiomatic implementation with thorough docstring, doctest-style examples, sensible input validation, and helpful usage demos, though extra validation exceeds task scope slightly.

challenges.name: readme-writer
challenges.description: Pure documentation ability — no code at all
challenges.notes: Comprehensive, well-organized README covering all required sections with correct flag documentation, clear examples, and proper Markdown/code-block usage, though it invents extra details (e.g., MD5, placeholder repo links) not specified in the prompt.

challenges.name: refactor
challenges.description: Code clarity + whether the model can explain its changes
challenges.notes: Accurate, well-documented refactor with meaningful names, idiomatic comprehension, correct type hints, and specific, non-generic explanations for each change.

challenges.name: binary-search
challenges.description: Algorithm + full docstring (Args/Returns/Raises + examples)
challenges.notes: Correct O(log n) binary search with overflow-safe mid calculation and solid edge-case handling, though the added O(n) sortedness check slightly undermines efficiency and the response ends with a truncated 'sample output' section; documentation is thorough with accurate docstring examples and meaningful comments.

challenges.name: async-fetch
challenges.description: Async concurrency patterns + retry/timeout handling
challenges.notes: Solid implementation with proper concurrency, per-request timeout, exponential backoff, and never-raise behavior, though the response is truncated mid-explanation and uses a slightly odd backoff scheme (0s on first attempt) plus TCPConnector(limit=0) which removes any concurrency control.'

challenges.name: elixir-test
challenges.description: ExUnit describe blocks + assert_raise + unicode handling
challenges.notes: patched after judge fix

challenges.name: sql
challenges.description: CTE + window function fluency on a real schema
challenges.notes: patched after judge fix

challenges.name: test-writing
challenges.description: Edge-case thinking + pytest parametrize discipline
challenges.notes: Well-designed, thorough parametrized happy-path and error tests with accurate arithmetic and clear naming, but the response is truncated mid-function ('with pyt'), producing a syntax error that would prevent the test suite from even being collected.

challenges.name: debug
challenges.description: Careful reading + correctness reasoning over broken code
challenges.notes: patched after judge fix

challenges.name: go-test
challenges.description: Idiomatic Go table-driven tests + benchmark
challenges.notes: Test cases and benchmark are well-designed and exceed the requirement, but the file incorrectly declares package wordcount_test and reimplements a stub WordCount that always returns nil, meaning the delivered file will fail most subtests unless manually edited to remove the stub and fix the package, contradicting the stated 'package: wordcount' requirement.

challenges.name: api-client
challenges.description: Class design + error handling + type hints + docs
challenges.notes: Response is truncated mid-method, missing the post() method entirely and the usage example, so the code doesn't even run as-is despite otherwise clean structure and good docstrings for what exists.

challenges.name: doom
challenges.description: DDA raycaster + textures + door + minimap + Z-buffer — the signature challenge
